examples: I feel nauseous after eating too much.
The boat ride made me nauseous.
She felt nauseous during the flight.
context: The line for this ride. We waited for about an hour and ended up bailing. It was too long and I couldn't wait. I hate spinny rides like this. They make me feel so dizzy and nauseous. Honestly, I want to puke just looking at it. Not for me. Let's go to the carousel. This is so cute. They call it the dairy goround instead of merrygoround because they have farm animals. That's adorable.

examples: This song makes me feel nostalgic.
I'm feeling nostalgic about my college days.
The old photos made us all nostalgic.
context: >> Nobody's here. We actually rented the park out today, so just me, you know, private experience. Just kidding. We need a lot more money for that. It's starting to rain pretty hard. Just my luck. How did the weather say 100% sunshine? And now we get this. I guess the clouds add to the spooky vibe. Let's stay positive. Is it a trip to Lay World if you don't rent a school uniform? I don't think so. Of course you are. We have to match. Look at the bloody uniforms. I know for a lot of Koreans this is very nostalgic, but for me it reminds me of Britney Spears, the hit me baby one more time video. She dresses in a school uniform. Very iconic. I feel like my biceps are going to bust out of this uniform. I got to take it easy on the protein. The sizes are stressing me out. It's either too small or too big.

examples: I'm a wimp when it comes to spicy food.
He called me a wimp for not jumping in the pool.
context: I was going to attempt it, but the wait is 50 minutes. It's just a bit too long for me. Secretly, I'm a little bit relieved. I know it's a bit lame, but my biggest fear is haunted houses. I'm a very jumpy person and I'm a huge wimp.
